title: Infrastructure question: VPS location vs provider for crypto algos
author: prob2584
selftext:
I am revisiting my infrastructure setup for crypto algorithmic trading and might want some perspective from people running live systems.

Situation:

* Multiple strategies running continuously
* Execution via exchange APIs
* Reliability for me is more important than raw compute power.

Up to now, I have used general cloud VPS. They work, but during busy market periods, I've noticed more jitter and occasional hiccups than I am comfortable with in the long term.

I've now started wondering whether migration to a trading-oriented VPS, hosted closer to exchange infrastructure is worth it, or if placement plus monitoring on a regular cloud instance would do.

I've seen people mention setups using providers like TradingVPS.io alongside standard cloud providers, but it's hard to separate actual execution benefits from anecdotal bias.

For those running crypto algos in production:

Was VPS type a factor, or was it purely a matter of location and stability of the network? or Any measurable increase in consistency after setup change?

Not looking for recommendations or promotions, just trying to make a better informed infrastructure decision before scaling.

comments.author: kotarel
comments.body: Won't matter for retail. You still get about 250ms of delay on bars at minimum on most brokers. You can cut down on the extra if you want but if you need that fast for your algo to work you need better data lines not VPS.

comments.author: OldSherman
comments.body:
Most hiccups during busy markets come from network congestion or noisy neighbors, not CPU.

Putting the VPS in the same region as the exchange reduced jitter more than upgrading specs.

Trading-focused VPSs help mainly because of colocation and predictable bandwidth, not execution magic.

The biggest consistency gains came from monitoring, auto-restarts, and solid API error handling.


A well-chosen cloud VPS in the right location with good ops often performs just as well.
On the execution side, once things scale, I’ve found tools like Rubic useful for cross-chain routing, but it’s just one piece.

comments.author: EndlessKnight_154
comments.body:
I’ve run crypto algos on both regular cloud VPS setups and trading focused ones, and the issues you’re describing sound familiar. Most of my problems weren’t power-related, it was latency spikes and random network hiccups during high vol moves.

What helped was getting hosting physically closer to the exchange and using a setup that prioritizes network stability. I’m using a trading VPS now (cheapforex for part of my stack), and the order flow has been noticeably smoother. If your strategy is timing sensitive, it’s worth testing side by side before you scale.

comments.author: Patient-Bumblebee
comments.body: Haven't used a VPS or run an algo since last year. My strategies are all text-based now (using a vibetrading DEX).
